title: coumarouna odorata var. tetraphylla
intro: Coumarouna odorata var. tetraphylla, also known as Copaiba, is a tropical tree native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the Copaifera genus. Its bark contains oleoresin, which has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Copaiba has been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Herbal preparations of Copaiba include essential oils, extracts, and resin, which can be used topically or internally, and are often used in aromatherapy and traditional medicine practices.
